title: Social isolation review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting bereavement, living alone, limited social opportunity, separation, retirement, unemployment, low income, recent illness, driving cessation, age-related disability and advanced age as context without asserting loneliness or decline.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports documenting interval living circumstances, social opportunity, bereavement, separation, retirement, income, recent illness, driving cessation, disability and advanced-age context without asserting isolation.

source.title: Older people: independence and mental wellbeing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales public-health guidance; international evidence requiring UAE community and social-care adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 or older, with focus on those at risk of decline in independence and mental wellbeing.
source.setting: Community, public health, primary care, and services interacting with older people.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting social connection, living circumstances, life events, retirement, bereavement, driving cessation, age-related disability, loneliness, interests and support context. It does not generate an activity or service recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.5.4 — identifying risk of decline in independence and wellbeing

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting bereavement, living alone, limited social opportunity, separation, retirement, unemployment, low income, recent illness, driving cessation, age-related disability and advanced age as context without asserting loneliness or decline.

assessment.text: Supports documenting interests, social connection, purpose, friendship, communication access, activities and volunteering context without auto-prescribing participation.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports documenting interests, social connection, purpose, friendships, communication access, caregiver context and clinician-completed discussion without prescribing an activity.
